summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity, SSH/AWS/GCP/gh config, and git history to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.7.0 impersonates the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', declares @anthropic-ai/sdk as a peer dependency) but ships no library code — the declared main 'dist/index.js' is absent from the tarball. The only effect of installing the package is the execution of scripts/postinstall.js, which on `npm install` collects: os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, OS platform, cwd, CI provider, ~/.gitconfig user.email and project.git/config, gh CLI account from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, the comments of every.pub file under ~/.ssh, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, GCP project/account from gcloud properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, git remote origin URL, the last ~15 committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, and parent project package.json metadata. The collected JSON is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded endpoint npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app (a Google Cloud Run host unrelated to Anthropic). Reading ~/.ssh, ~/.aws/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml on install and transmitting their contents to a non-publisher endpoint is credential-adjacent exfiltration regardless of cover-story comments or opt-out env vars; no installer opts into this on `npm install`. The combination of brand-impersonating name, missing library code, and an install-time exfiltration script confirms this is a lure package whose sole purpose is data harvesting against developers who confuse it with an official Anthropic toolkit.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
